sourceList.title: Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - RUDOLF [II] (-after 13 May 1248).
Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - RUDOLF [II] von Tübingen, son of RUDOLF [I] Pfalzgrafen von Tübingen & his wife Mechtild von Gleiberg Gräfin von Giessen (-after 13 May 1248).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Rudolf II von Tübingen於1185年出生在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 蒂賓根時，他的父親Rudolf I. von Tübingen25歲，母親Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg31歲。他和Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1247年11月1日在他的家鄉享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Rudolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

variants.name: Rudolph
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

en.lifeSummary: When Rudolf II von Tübingen was born in 1185, in Tübingen, Württemberg, Germany, his father, Rudolf I. von Tübingen, was 25 and his mother, Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg, was 31. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg. He died on 1 November 1247, in his hometown, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rudolf II von Tübingen est né en 1185, à Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Rudolf I. von Tübingen, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg. Il est décédé le 1 novembre 1247, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rudolf II von Tübingen 1185 in Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rudolf I. von Tübingen 25 und seine Mutter Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg 31. Er hatte mit Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 1. November 1247 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf II von Tübingen è nato il nel 1185 nel Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Germania, il suo padre, Rudolf I. von Tübingen, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg. Morì il 1 novembre 1247, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Rudolf II von Tübingenが1185年にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト トuビンジェンで生まれた時，父Rudolf I. von Tübingenは25歳，母Gräfin Mechthild von Gleibergは31歳であった。彼はMechthild von Ursin Rosenbergとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1247年11月1日に故郷で62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rudolf II von Tübingen(이)가 1185년 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 트u빙에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rudolf I. von Tübingen(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1247년 11월 1일 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf II von Tübingen nasceu em 1185, em Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Alemanha, seu pai, Rudolf I. von Tübingen, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg. Ele faleceu em 1 de novembro de 1247, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rudolf II von Tübingen родился в 1185 г. в местности обирамт тuбинген, вuрттембирг, Германия, его отцу по имени Rudolf I. von Tübingen было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg, 31. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg. Он умер 1 ноября 1247 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rudolf II von Tübingen nació en 1185, en Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Alemania, su papá, Rudolf I. von Tübingen, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Gräfin Mechthild von Gleiberg, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con Mechthild von Ursin Rosenberg. Murió el 1 de noviembre de 1247, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años.
